text:
Once upon a time in a quiet village, there lived a peculiar bird named Minerva. Unlike her fellow songbirds, she didn't sing. Instead, she delighted in pondering the mysteries of the world.

Minerva had no friend. She could talk, quite mellifensively. She would sit on her little branch singing about the seasons, the stars, the quiet hum of the evening insects.

One day, while lazily fluttering around her favorite tulip field, Minerva heard something. It wasn’t talking; it was just there. A hedgehog!

She swooped down in excitement and hugged the little creature. It squeaked, astonished. It hadn't seen anything like her before.

"You always could find something so interesting," Minerva chuckled. She hadn't realized her feather had such dextrous claws.

The hedgehog visited frequently, every afternoon, exchanging gossip about the squirrels.

One day, Minerva discovered a nest behind a bush. She wasn't sure what kind
